description: Per-user configuration for follow-up nudge behavior. One row per user. Stores follow-up delay periods by recipient type, master enable/disable, and the managed snoozed folder ID.

alternateKeys.purpose: Ensures one configuration row per user. Enables safe Upsert to create default config on first use without race conditions. Uses cr_owneruserid instead of ownerid because Dataverse does not support ownerid in alternate keys.

columns.description: The systemuserid GUID of the owning user. Used as alternate key since ownerid is not supported in Dataverse alternate keys.
Primary column. Auto-populated with the user's display name + ' Nudge Config' for readability in views.
Business days to wait before nudging for internal (same-domain) recipients.
Business days to wait before nudging for external recipients.
Business days to wait before nudging for priority contacts.
Business days to wait before nudging for general/uncategorized recipients.
Master on/off switch for follow-up nudges. When false, the Sent Items Tracker still logs emails but the Response Detection flow skips this user.
Graph folder ID of the user's managed EPA-Snoozed folder. Created on first use by the Snooze Detection flow. Null until Phase 2 (Snooze Auto-Removal) is deployed.

notes.cardinality: Exactly one row per user. The alternate key on cr_owneruserid enforces this. Default values are applied on first creation. Note: cr_owneruserid duplicates the ownerid value because Dataverse does not support ownerid in alternate keys.
notes.recipient_type_mapping: The Sent Items Tracker flow determines recipient type by: (1) checking against a priority contact list (future feature), (2) comparing recipient domain to user's tenant domain (Internal vs External), (3) defaulting to General.
notes.business_day_calculation: Business days exclude weekends (Saturday, Sunday) using dayOfWeek() in Power Automate expressions. Holiday exclusion is explicitly out of scope for MVP.
notes.configuration_surface: Users configure their nudge settings via a Canvas App settings screen. Direct Dataverse editing is not supported for end users.
notes.security: UserOwned table with ownership-based RLS. Users can only read/write their own configuration row.
notes.system_columns: Standard Dataverse system columns (ownerid, createdon, modifiedon, statecode, statuscode) are created automatically.
